title: Java Try With Resources

completionChecks.prompt: What happens to reader when the try-with-resources block exits, even if readLine throws?
choices: It stays open until it is garbage collected
It is closed only if no exception was thrown
It must be closed manually in the catch block
It is closed automatically, before the catch block runs
completionChecks.explanation: A resource declared in the try header is closed automatically when control leaves the block, including on an exception, and that close runs before the matching catch.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the catch block do when readLine throws IOException?
choices: It swallows the IOException and continues
It wraps the IOException in an IllegalStateException and throws that
It returns null from the method
completionChecks.explanation: The catch converts the checked IOException into an unchecked IllegalStateException(e), preserving the original as the cause, and throws it.

completionChecks.prompt: If reader.readLine() throws IOException and reader.close() also throws IOException, what is thrown by this code?
choices: Only the close IOException is thrown, because closing happens last
An IllegalStateException whose cause is the readLine IOException, with the close IOException suppressed
A plain NullPointerException from the catch block
completionChecks.explanation: The try failure remains the primary IOException, the close failure is suppressed on it, and the catch wraps the primary exception.
